headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the sharpest criticism, while Anthropic received mixed praise and complaints, and Google Gemini and Microsoft Copilot saw limited negative remarks [E7][E1][E3][E8].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was discussed negatively, with comments calling Codex’s output bad even on basic tasks and condemning the company alongside current LLMs [E7][E9].
Google Gemini trended negative on limited evidence, with remarks about tool-use loops and a comparison suggesting its style has become less distinctive [E3][E6].
Microsoft Copilot had limited evidence for a negative lean, driven by a complaint that Office 365 Copilot is annoying and steals focus with repeated popups [E8].

Anthropic was mixed but slightly positive overall: some comments praised transparency and Opus quality, while others criticized Claude for high token costs, repetitive style, and weaker reliability [E1][E2][E4][E5][E10].

evidence.excerpt: Go Anthropic for transparency and commitment to science.

annotations.rationale: Direct praise for Anthropic's transparency and scientific approach.

evidence.excerpt: I noticed I already had blown through about $5 of Claude tokens in half an hour, and deleted the VPS immediately. Then today I saw this follow up: https://mastodon.macstories.net/@viticci/115968901926545907 - the author blew through $560 of tokens in a weekend of playing with it. If you want to run this full time to organise your mailbox and your agenda, it's probably cheaper to hire a real human personal assistant.

annotations.rationale: The commenter complains that Claude token costs are so high that using the product full-time seems uneconomical.

evidence.excerpt: It’s actually the only agent I could get Gemini 3 Flash and Pro to consistently use tools with without going into loops.

annotations.rationale: This implies Gemini models have tool-use reliability issues and tend to loop unless paired with a specific agent.

evidence.excerpt: Almost all slopware made using claude have the same color palette. So much for a random token generator to be this consistent

annotations.rationale: The commenter criticizes Claude-generated outputs as recognizable 'slopware' with an undesirable, repetitive visual style.

evidence.excerpt: It was significantly less focused and reliable than Opus 4.5 at implementing some mostly-fleshed-out specs I had lying around for some needed modifications to an enterprise TS node/express service. I was a bit disappointed tbh, the speed via fireworks.ai is great, they're doing great work on the hosting side. But I found the model had to double-back to fix type issues, broken tests, etc, far more than Opus 4.5 which churned through the tasks with almost zero errors. In fact, I gave the resulting code to Opus, simply said it looked "sloppy" and Opus cleaned it up very quickly.

annotations.rationale: Opus 4.5 is described as more focused and reliable, with almost zero errors and fast cleanup of sloppy code.

evidence.excerpt: talks in more ChatGPT/Gemini/C-3PO style now. It's not explictly bad, I'm sure most people won't care but it was something that made it unique so it's a shame to see it go.

annotations.rationale: The commenter uses 'ChatGPT' style as a mildly unfavorable comparison, implying bland or less distinctive output.
The commenter uses 'Gemini' style as a mildly unfavorable comparison, implying bland or less distinctive output.

evidence.excerpt: I asked Codex to write some unit tests for Redux today. At first glance it looked fine, and I continued on. I then went back to add a test by hand, and after looking more closely at the output there were like 50 wtf worthy things scattered in there. Sure they ran, but it was bad in all sorts of ways. And this was just writing something very basic.

annotations.rationale: Codex is described as producing obviously bad, 'wtf worthy' output even for a basic testing task.

evidence.excerpt: One of the more annoying software that does this is the copilot Office 365 on the web. Every time (!) I open it, it shows a popup on how to add files to the context. That itself would be annoying, but it also steals focus!

annotations.rationale: The commenter calls Office 365 Copilot annoying because of repetitive popups and focus-stealing behavior.

evidence.excerpt: LLMs are a scourge on humanity as they are currently implemented, and what will they do when these are no longer needed? I can't wait until OpenAI, NVIDIA and Microsoft all go belly up.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly calls current LLMs a 'scourge on humanity' and says they want OpenAI to fail.

evidence.excerpt: it's the first time I've felt that an open source model really competes with the big labs. So far it's been able to handle most things I've thrown at it. I'm almost hesitant to say that this is as good as Opus.

annotations.rationale: Using Opus as the standard for top-tier performance implies positive regard for Anthropic's model quality.
